mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that compress corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in consumer price inflation indicate robust demand without pricing pressure, which expands profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive rank IC greater than 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, driving positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls consistently correlate with expanding consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 60-day returns following inflation spikes above 0.4 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and real GDP signals synchronized expansion across residential and aggregate demand sectors, which sustains earnings momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP growth signal produces negative forward returns in over 60 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden contraction in retail sales growth below 0.2 percent month-over-month indicates immediate consumer demand weakness, triggering rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2 percent
failure_condition: Retail sales contractions fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 48 percent in walk-forward tests.
